Signs of Meteors XXXIII 309
am ai i

The Tar^ or shooting star is one cubit long, white, red or
slender like the fibre of the lotus-stalk and moves across, up and
down the sky, as if being dragged.
[Cf. the abridged work :—
am 5 ferar faar ami n]
ftRftr fermr i
a vrafa ysa w\\
The Ulka or meteor is broad at the head, having a tiny tail.
As it falls down, it grows bigger and bigger. It is 3J cubits long.
There are many varieties of it.
[Cf. the following :
TOfrnra> farrar agsRarr n]
Those varieties of Ulka that resemble dead bodies, weapons,
asses, camels, crocodiles, monkeys, boars, ploughs, wild deer,
lizards, snakes and smoke, as well as those that have two heads, are
of inauspicious consequences.
ftra^fwr: u?on
